title: Boss Bars (1.9+)
description: A complete deep-dive into boss bars including creation, lifecycle management, schedulers, memory safety, and real-world usage.

lessons.title: Creating a Boss Bar (FULL GUIDE)
lessons.content:
# Creating a Boss Bar (FULL GUIDE)

## Step 1: WHERE THIS CODE GOES

Put this inside:
- A command
- OR an event (like PlayerJoinEvent)

Example: inside onCommand()

---

## Step 2: CODE

```java
BossBar bar = Bukkit.createBossBar("§6Defeat the Dragon!", BarColor.RED, BarStyle.SOLID);

bar.addPlayer(player);
bar.setProgress(1.0);
```

---

## Step 3: FULL BREAKDOWN

createBossBar(title, color, style)
→ creates boss bar object

§6
→ color code (gold)

BarColor.RED
→ bar color

BarStyle.SOLID
→ smooth bar

addPlayer(player)
→ SHOWS bar (VERY IMPORTANT)

setProgress(1.0)
→ full bar (range = 0.0 → 1.0)

---

## Step 4: WHAT HAPPENS IN-GAME

Player sees a red boss bar at top of screen

---

## Step 5: HUGE BEGINNER MISTAKE

❌ Forgetting addPlayer()
→ bar exists but NOT visible

---

## Step 6: LIFECYCLE CONCEPT (IMPORTANT)

BossBar is NOT temporary

YOU must:
- remove players
- or reuse it

---

## Mini Challenge
Create a blue boss bar with 50% progress

## Recap
Create → Add player → Set progress

quiz.question: Which method shows the boss bar?

lessons.title: Updating Boss Bars (Schedulers Explained)
lessons.content:
# Updating Boss Bars (FULL GUIDE)

## Step 1: WHERE THIS GOES

Inside command OR event

Requires plugin instance

---

## Step 2: CODE

```java
new BukkitRunnable() {
    double progress = 1.0;

    @Override
    public void run() {
        progress -= 0.05;

        if (progress <= 0) {
            bar.removePlayer(player);
            cancel();
            return;
        }

        bar.setProgress(progress);
        bar.setTitle("§cTime left: " + (int)(progress * 20) + "s");
    }
}.runTaskTimer(plugin, 0L, 20L);
```

---

## Step 3: FULL BREAKDOWN

BukkitRunnable
→ repeating task

run()
→ runs every interval

runTaskTimer(plugin, delay, period)
→ scheduler

0L
→ start instantly

20L
→ every 1 second (20 ticks)

progress -= 0.05
→ decreases bar

cancel()
→ STOPS task (IMPORTANT)

---

## Step 4: WHAT HAPPENS

Boss bar slowly decreases like a timer

---

## Step 5: CRITICAL DEV CONCEPT

Scheduler = how plugins create time-based systems

Used for:
- cooldowns
- timers
- animations

---

## Step 6: COMMON MISTAKES

❌ Not cancelling → infinite task (lag)
❌ Updating every tick → unnecessary lag

---

## Mini Challenge
Make it last 10 seconds instead

## Recap
Scheduler → update → cancel

lessons.title: Colors, Styles & Visual Design
lessons.content:
# Boss Bar Styles (FULL GUIDE)

## Step 1: CODE

```java
BossBar bar = Bukkit.createBossBar("§aChallenge", BarColor.GREEN, BarStyle.SEGMENTED_10);
```

---

## Step 2: FULL BREAKDOWN

BarColor:
- RED
- GREEN
- BLUE
- YELLOW
- PURPLE
- WHITE

BarStyle:
- SOLID
- SEGMENTED_6
- SEGMENTED_10
- SEGMENTED_12
- SEGMENTED_20

---

## Step 3: WHAT SEGMENTS MEAN

SEGMENTED_10
→ bar splits into 10 chunks

Used for:
- boss phases
- health stages

---

## Step 4: DESIGN TIP (IMPORTANT)

GOOD:
- Red = danger
- Green = success

BAD:
- random colors (confusing)

---

## Step 5: COMMON MISTAKE

❌ Too many boss bars
→ clutters player screen

---

## Mini Challenge
Make a segmented health bar

## Recap
Color = meaning
Style = visuals

lessons.title: Boss Bar Lifecycle (ADVANCED & IMPORTANT)
lessons.content:
# Boss Bar Lifecycle (FULL GUIDE)

## Step 1: WHY THIS MATTERS

BossBars stay in memory

If you DON'T clean them up → memory leaks

---

## Step 2: REMOVE PLAYER

```java
bar.removePlayer(player);
```

---

## Step 3: REMOVE ALL

```java
bar.removeAll();
```

---

## Step 4: WHERE TO CLEAN UP

PlayerQuitEvent:

```java
@EventHandler
public void onQuit(PlayerQuitEvent event) {
    bar.removePlayer(event.getPlayer());
}
```

---

## Step 5: WHAT HAPPENS IF YOU DON'T

❌ Ghost boss bars
❌ Memory leaks
❌ Weird visual bugs

---

## Step 6: PRO DEV TIP

Store boss bars in a HashMap:

```java
Map<Player, BossBar> bars = new HashMap<>();
```

So you can manage them

---

## Mini Challenge
Store and remove boss bars per player

## Recap
Create → use → REMOVE
